description:
A distributed-systems incident review has begun and you, the data analyst on duty, have
    been asked to produce a *very small* diagnostic summary from an existing cluster
    event-stream.

    1. Source data  
       • Location: /home/user/data/logs.csv  
       • Format: standard UTF-8 CSV, comma-separated, UNIX line-feeds.  
       • The first row is a header.  
       • Columns (in order): timestamp,node_id,level,message.  
       • The field “level” may hold INFO, WARN or ERROR.  

    2. Required output  
       • Create a **new** file at /home/user/output/error_summary.csv.  
       • The file must contain exactly **four** lines:  
         1. a header  
         2. one line per unique node_id that appears in level=ERROR rows.  
       • Exact layout, including the header, must be:

             node_id,error_count
             node-1,3
             node-2,2
             node-3,1

         (Each line must end with a single newline character; there must be *no*
         blank lines, leading/trailing whitespace, tab characters, or extra
         columns.)

       • Lines after the header must be sorted in plain ASCII/lexicographical
         order by node_id (node-1 before node-2 before node-3).  
       • The output directory /home/user/output must exist when you finish; create
         it if it is not already there.  
       • If /home/user/output/error_summary.csv already exists, overwrite it.  

    3. Constraints  
       • Accomplish the aggregation with **one** compound shell command entered at
         the terminal (using pipes is fine; the entire job should execute as a
         single line once you press <Enter>).  
       • Do not open an interactive editor.  
       • You do not need elevated privileges; everything should run as the
         ordinary “user” account.  

    Once the file is written, display its contents in the terminal so that the
    incident commander can immediately see the result.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing dataset -->
    <file path="/home/user/data/logs.csv"><![CDATA[
timestamp,node_id,level,message
2023-01-01T00:00:00Z,node-1,INFO,Service started
2023-01-01T00:01:00Z,node-2,ERROR,Unable to reach DB
2023-01-01T00:02:00Z,node-1,ERROR,Timeout
2023-01-01T00:03:00Z,node-2,WARN,High memory
2023-01-01T00:04:00Z,node-3,ERROR,Disk full
2023-01-01T00:05:00Z,node-1,INFO,Service heartbeat
2023-01-01T00:06:00Z,node-2,ERROR,Connection reset
2023-01-01T00:07:00Z,node-3,INFO,Service started
2023-01-01T00:08:00Z,node-1,ERROR,Out of memory
2023-01-01T00:09:00Z,node-1,ERROR,Service crashed
]]></file>

    <!-- Expected artefact -->
    <expected_file path="/home/user/output/error_summary.csv"><![CDATA[
node_id,error_count
node-1,3
node-2,2
node-3,1
]]></expected_file>
